title: acer amplum subsp. catalpifolium
intro: Acer amplum subsp. catalpifolium, also known as the catalpa-leafed maple, is a subspecies of the Acer amplum tree, native to certain regions in China. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, making it useful for treating various ailments. Traditionally, the leaves of the catalpa-leafed maple can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
